zh.lifeSummary: 當Cecily de Fontaines於大約1180年出生在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪時，她的父親Aléaume de Fontaines19歲，母親Laurette Saint Valéry18歲。她與Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell在英國英格蘭達勒姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1227年2月2日在英國達勒姆巴納德 卡素享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Cecily de Fontaines于大约1180年出生在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪时，她的父亲Aléaume de Fontaines19岁，母亲Laurette Saint Valéry18岁。她与Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell在英国英格兰達勒姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1227年2月2日在英国達勒姆巴納德 卡素享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Fontaines
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Normand, Pierre, Emile, Fernand, Lucien, Marcel, Adelard, Henri, Jacques, Laurent.

French and Walloon: topographic name for someone living near a spring or well, from Old French fontaine, from Late Latin fontana, a derivative of Latin fons (see Font ); or a habitational name from Fontaine, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Fountaine .
Shortened form of French   Lafontaine  , a variant of 1 above.

variants.name: Fontenot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Ulysse, Andrus, Alcide, Andre, Edouard, Lucien, Marise, Monique, Remy.

French: variant of Fonteneau (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from any of several places mainly in the westcentral part of France so named. The surname Fontenot is very rare in France.
History: Jean-Louis Fonteneau/Fontenot from Montierneuf in Vienne, France, married Louise-Angélique Henry in Mobile, AL, in 1726.

variants.name: Fontenelle
meanings: French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from (La) Fontenelle, the name of several places in various parts of France, of the same origin.

variants.name: Fontenette
meanings: French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from Fontenette, the name of several places mainly in the central part of France, of the same origin.

variants.name: Fountain
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from one or more of the places in France named Fontaine, Fonteyne, or Lafontaine (see   Fontaine  ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a spring or well, from Middle English fontayne (Old French fontaine) ‘spring, fountain’, sometimes with a plural -s, and synonymous with Well , of which Fountain(es) may sometimes have been a translation.

variants.name: Font
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Miguel, Rafael, Eduardo, Raul, Eugenio, Pedro, Roberto, Aida.
Catalan and southern French: topographic name for someone living near a spring or well, Catalan and Occitan font ‘spring, well’ (from Latin fons, genitive fontis).
English: variant of   Fant  .

variants.name: Fontan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Angel, Benigno, Carlos, Jose, Juana, Manuel, Pedro, Adolfo, Aida, Alfredo, Candido, Emilia.
Spanish and Galician (Fontán): habitational name from any of the many places called Fontán, from fontán ‘spring’.
French: variant of   Fontaine  .

nameMeanings.name: Cecily
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cecilie
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cicely
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Cecily de Fontaines was born about 1180, in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, her father, Aléaume de Fontaines, was 19 and her mother, Laurette Saint Valéry, was 18. She married Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell in Durham, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died about 2 February 1227, in Barnard Castle, Durham, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Cecily de Fontaines est née environ 1180, à Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, son père, Aléaume de Fontaines, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Laurette Saint Valéry, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell à Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée environ 2 février 1227, à Barnard Castle, Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Cecily de Fontaines um 1180 in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Aléaume de Fontaines 19 und ihre Mutter Laurette Saint Valéry 18. Sie hat in Durham, England, United Kingdom Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb um 2. Februar 1227 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Barnard Castle, Durham, England.

Lei sposato Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell nel Durham, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì circa 2 febbraio 1227, nel Barnard Castle, Durham, England, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Cecily de Fontainesが約1180年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアアバーディーンで生まれた時，父Aléaume de Fontainesは19歳，母Laurette Saint Valéryは18歳であった。イギリスイギリスダルハムでLord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1227年2月2日にイギリスダルハムバーナルド カストルで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Cecily de Fontaines(이)가 대략 1180년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 압르디에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Aléaume de Fontaines(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Laurette Saint Valéry(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람에서 Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1227년 2월 2일 영국 뒤람 바른아르드 카스틀에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell em Durham, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 2 de fevereiro de 1227, em Barnard Castle, Durham, Inglaterra, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cecily de Fontaines родилась около 1180 г. в местности абирдин, абирдиншир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Aléaume de Fontaines было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Laurette Saint Valéry, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord Hugh de Balliol Of Bywell в местности дурам, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла около 2 февраля 1227 г. в местности барнард кастл, дурам, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.
